market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: energy (+23.2% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+54.4% YTD) | 1 secteur(s) en refroidissement (3M ≤ -2.0%) | 52W leader: information-technology (+64.6% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: energy (+27.1% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: financials, communication-services, healthcare | Régions en difficulté: india | Concentration: peu de secteurs en zone favorable (rotation possible)
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.25,d=-0.04%), energy(favored,b=0.15,d=0.42%), industrials(neutral,b=0.92,d=-1.22%), materials(neutral,b=0.75,d=-0.84%), financials(neutral,b=0.96,d=-0.15%), consumer-discretionary(neutral,b=1.24,d=-0.04%), communication-services(neutral,b=1.24,d=0.55%), healthcare(neutral,b=0.6,d=-0.83%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.44,d=-1.74%), information-technology(neutral,b=1.44,d=0.07%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Brent near $99 is a significant energy shock, PCE at 2.8% well above target with breakevens at 2.61%, while Fed is on hold (0 cuts in 6m) suggesting they're stuck — growth is decelerating (RADAR shows few favored sectors, financials/healthcare struggling) while inflation remains sticky. Not full stagflation yet but the mix of elevated oil, above-target inflation, and a cautious Fed with VIX at 22 points toward a stagflationary tilt rather than goldilocks.

adjustments.action: Increase energy/oil cap — Brent near $99, energy leading +23% YTD with strong 6M momentum
adjustments.rationale: Brent near $100 with energy sector leading all timeframes; energy is the natural stagflation hedge and momentum confirms the macro thesis.
Even conservative portfolios need some inflation protection via real assets when oil is near $100.

adjustments.action: Reduce AI/semi exposure — high-beta growth vulnerable to sticky inflation and rate uncertainty
adjustments.rationale: IT sector is 52W leader (+64.6%) but daily momentum is flat (+0.07%); with PCE at 2.8% and Fed on hold, duration-sensitive growth faces headwinds from rates staying higher for longer.

adjustments.action: Increase gold allocation — gold at $4697 with 11.8% drawdown from ATH is a buying opportunity in stagflationary regime
adjustments.rationale: Gold's 12% drawdown from ATH in a regime of $99 oil, sticky inflation, and geopolitical risk creates an attractive entry; gold is the canonical stagflation asset.

adjustments.action: Increase dividend/value tilt — defensive quality outperforms in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: Consumer staples and low-beta defensives provide inflation pass-through and earnings stability when growth decelerates under energy cost pressure.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS, shorten duration, avoid HY — stagflation playbook
adjustments.rationale: PCE 2.8% + breakevens 2.61% means real rates are thin; TIPS protect purchasing power while short duration limits mark-to-market risk if Fed is forced to hike.

adjustments.action: Raise bond floor for Stable — increase defensive allocation in uncertain regime
adjustments.rationale: Stable portfolio at 36% bonds is light for a stagflationary tilt; increasing floor to ~39% via short TIPS provides better risk-adjusted protection.

adjustments.action: Trim healthcare cap — sector in difficulty per RADAR despite defensive nature
adjustments.rationale: Healthcare daily -0.83% and flagged as struggling by RADAR; in stagflation, prefer energy and gold over healthcare which faces margin pressure from input costs.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Short-duration TIPS-heavy strategy: inflation protection is paramount with PCE at 2.8% and oil near $100; avoid credit risk as HY spreads at 285bps look tight for a potential slowdown and could widen sharply.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX at 22 with stagflationary tilt warrants modest cash buffer to buy dips if oil shock escalates or equity corrects; not excessive since T-bills (CLTL, GBIL already in portfolios) serve as productive cash.

ai_response._cash_guidance: MINIMUM CASH: Agressif 2% if VIX>20 (option d'achat sur la vol), Modéré 3%, Stable 0% (T-bills = cash productif). Le cash est une POSITION, pas une absence de position.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Crypto is a risk asset that correlates with liquidity; with Fed on hold and stagflationary pressures, reduce crypto caps — not zero for Agressif as BTC has some digital-gold narrative but cap it conservatively.

warnings: OIL SUPPLY SHOCK ESCALATION: Brent at $99 is near the $100 psychological level — a break above could trigger second-round inflation effects forcing Fed to hike, which would be severely negative for all risk assets.

FED POLICY ERROR RISK: Fed on hold with PCE at 2.8% — if they stay too long, inflation expectations could de-anchor (breakevens already 2.61%); if they hike, recession risk spikes with already-weak sectors.

CREDIT SPREAD COMPLACENCY: HY at 285bps is historically tight for a stagflationary environment — potential for sharp repricing if growth data deteriorates, especially with LQD already down -0.11% today.
GOLD DRAWDOWN TRAP: Gold at -11.8% from ATH could be profit-taking or regime shift; if dollar strengthens materially (DTWEXBGS currently 118, near neutral), gold could fall further before recovering.

EQUITY CONCENTRATION RISK: S&P up 1.6% today but RADAR shows only energy/utilities favored with financials, healthcare, comm-services struggling — narrow leadership is fragile and vulnerable to rotation.

market_interpretation._doc: Champs interprétatifs dérivés des données brutes. L'AI déduit ce que les API ne fournissent pas.
